methods.method_name: Lab-Scale Sediment Simulation Setup and Incubation
methods.description: Setting up and running lab-scale reactors to simulate sediment conditions under control and risk scenarios, including sample collection during the incubation period. This method aims to replicate real reservoir conditions to study the impact of organic pollution on sediment processes.

steps.description: The investigated sediments and water from the reservoir to the HEPP "Lakatnik" were taken during September 2011.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect environmental samples (sediment, water) from a reservoir

steps.description: Two situations near to the real conditions were studied: 1) at a decreased concentration of organic pollutants (COD was 0.265 g O 2 •kg −1 ) in the case of discharge of sediments downstream of the river (control situation);
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare control sample with specific decreased organic pollutant concentration (COD)

steps.description: 2) at an accumulation of organic pollutants (COD was 1.463 g O 2 •kg −1 ) in the sediments by addition of dry whey in concentration 2 g•kg −1 (risk event).The whey as a model pollutant was used because it contained organics such as proteins and lactose.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare risk sample with accumulated organic pollutants by adding dry whey to achieve a specific COD

steps.description: The volume of the sediments to the volume of water was 1:1.The model process was accomplished in sealed airtight conical tubes with a total volume 50 ml.
steps.searchable_summary: Assemble experimental reactors using sealed airtight conical tubes with a 1:1 sediment to water ratio

steps.description: Twenty one reactors were used for every variant with three repetitions for every hour of sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare multiple replicate reactors (21 per variant, 3 per sampling hour) for each condition

steps.description: The reactors were placed in the thermostat in dark conditions for the photosynthesis inhibition because in the real situation on the bottom of lake this process isn't accomplished.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate reactors in dark conditions using a thermostat

steps.description: The reactors were incubated at a temperature 19˚C ± 2˚C.
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain incubation temperature at 19C +/- 2C

steps.description: The duration of the simulated process was 190 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: Run the incubation experiment for 190 hours

steps.description: Twenty one reactors were used for every variant with three repetitions for every hour of sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect samples from three independent repetitions for every hour of sampling during the 190-hour period

methods.description: Initial processing of collected samples to prepare them for subsequent chemical and enzymatic analyses, including centrifugation for chemical analysis and ultrasonic disintegration for enzyme activity determination.

steps.description: The pretreatment of the samples for quantitative determination of COD, proteins and lactose included centrifugation (1000 rpm•min −1 for 15 min.) in order to remove the sample turbidity.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge samples at 1000 rpm for 15 minutes to remove turbidity for chemical analysis

steps.description: The supernatant was analyzed spectrophotometrically.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect the supernatant from the centrifuged samples for spectrophotometric analysis

steps.description: The treatment of the samples for determination of the enzyme activities included ultra sonic disintegration of samples (3 × 10 sec.with frequency 22 kHz and vibration amplitude 8 µm) with UD-20 automatic (Techpan) for obtaining of a homogenous microbial suspension.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ultrasonic disintegration on samples (3x10 sec, 22 kHz, 8 µm) for enzyme activity analysis

steps.description: for obtaining of a homogenous microbial suspension.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain a homogenous microbial suspension after ultrasonic disintegration

methods.description: Quantitative determination of key organic pollutants (COD, proteins, lactose) in the collected supernatant samples using standard spectrophotometric methods.

steps.description: The COD was analyzed according to a standard procedure [14].
steps.searchable_summary: Measure Chemical Oxygen Demand (COD) in supernatant according to a standard procedure

steps.description: The protein concentration was determined by Microbiuret method [15]
steps.searchable_summary: Measure protein concentration in supernatant using the Microbiuret method

steps.description: and the lactose concentration according to Miller [16].
steps.searchable_summary: Measure lactose concentration in supernatant according to Miller method

methods.description: Determination of the activities of specific enzymes (anDHA, PRT, GAL, PAI) related to organic matter biotransformation using established methods.

steps.description: The phosphatase activity index (PAI) was an average value from the activities of acid, neutral and alkaline phosphatases which were analyzed according to Matavulj et al. [17].
steps.searchable_summary: Measure acid phosphatase activity according to Matavulj et al.

steps.description: The phosphatase activity index (PAI) was an average value from the activities of acid, neutral and alkaline phosphatases which were analyzed according to Matavulj et al. [17].
steps.searchable_summary: Measure neutral phosphatase activity according to Matavulj et al.

steps.description: The phosphatase activity index (PAI) was an average value from the activities of acid, neutral and alkaline phosphatases which were analyzed according to Matavulj et al. [17].
steps.searchable_summary: Measure alkaline phosphatase activity according to Matavulj et al.

steps.description: The phosphatase activity index (PAI) was an average value from the activities of acid, neutral and alkaline phosphatases
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Phosphatase Activity Index (PAI) as the average of acid, neutral, and alkaline phosphatase activities

steps.description: The protease activity was determined using a method of Ladd and Butler [18].
steps.searchable_summary: Measure protease activity using the method of Ladd and Butler

steps.description: The anaerobic dehydrogenase activity was measured by method with 2-(р-iodophenyl)-3-(р-nitrophenyl)-5-phenyl tetrazolium chloride (INT) [19].
steps.searchable_summary: Measure anaerobic dehydrogenase activity (anDHA) using the INT method

steps.description: The ß-galactosidase activity was investigated according to Miller [20].
steps.searchable_summary: Measure ß-galactosidase activity according to Miller method

methods.description: Presenting enzyme activity data per unit protein to account for variations in microbial biomass, using protein concentration determined by the Microbiuret method.

steps.description: The data for the enzyme activities were presented for unit protein [15].
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve measured enzyme activity data

steps.description: The data for the enzyme activities were presented for unit protein [15].
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve measured protein concentration data

steps.description: The data for the enzyme activities were presented for unit protein [15].
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize enzyme activity by dividing by protein concentration
outputs: Normalized enzyme activity data (activity per unit protein)

methods.description: Determining the risk effect (E_R) of the risk situation on enzyme activities compared to the control situation and interpreting the result to understand the stimulating or inhibiting impact of pollutants.

steps.description: The risk effect (E R ) was determined as a ratio of the enzyme activity for the risk situation (EA Risk ) to the enzyme activity for the control (EA Control ).
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve enzyme activity data for the risk situation (EA_Risk)

steps.description: The risk effect (E R ) was determined as a ratio of the enzyme activity for the risk situation (EA Risk ) to the enzyme activity for the control (EA Control ).
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve enzyme activity data for the control situation (EA_Control)

steps.description: Equation ( 1) was used for calculation of the risk effect.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate risk effect (E_R) as the ratio of EA_Risk to EA_Control

steps.description: IF E R was 1.0, the risk event had no effect on the enzyme activity.IF E R was below 1.0, the risk event had negative (inhibiting) effect on the enzyme activity.IF E R was above 1.0, the risk event had positive (stimulating) effect on the enzyme activity.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret E_R values to determine the type of effect (no effect, inhibiting, stimulating)

methods.method_name: Risk Evaluation based on Pollutant Concentration
methods.description: Evaluating the environmental risk by comparing the concentrations of organic pollutants (COD, proteins, lactose) in the risk and control situations at the end of the simulation, considering the lack of specific environmental legislation for sediments.

steps.description: The Risk Evaluation was based on comparison of pollutants concentration for the control and for the risk situation at the end of the process
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve end-point pollutant concentrations (COD, proteins, lactose) for the risk situation

steps.description: The Risk Evaluation was based on comparison of pollutants concentration for the control and for the risk situation at the end of the process
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve end-point pollutant concentrations (COD, proteins, lactose) for the control situation

steps.description: The Risk Evaluation was based on comparison of pollutants concentration for the control and for the risk situation at the end of the process
steps.searchable_summary: Compare pollutant concentrations between risk and control situations at the end point

steps.description: The Risk Evaluation was based on comparison of pollutants concentration for the control and for the risk situation at the end of the process because of absence of environmental legislation about maximal emission concentrations of pollutants in sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate environmental risk based on pollutant concentration comparison, considering legislative context

methods.method_name: Correlation Analysis for Early Warning Indicators
methods.description: Exploring potential indicators for early warning systems by analyzing the correlation between chemical and enzymological data obtained from the risk modeling using Linear Regression Analysis and Pearson's correlation coefficient.

steps.description: The obtained data from risk modelling were used.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve chemical data (COD, proteins, lactose) from risk modeling experiment

steps.description: The obtained data from risk modelling were used.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve enzymological data (anDHA, PRT, GAL, PAI) from risk modeling experiment

steps.description: Linear Regression Analysis for determination of the Pearson's correlation coefficient (r) between chemical and enzymological data was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform linear regression analysis between chemical and enzymological data

steps.description: Linear Regression Analysis for determination of the Pearson's correlation coefficient (r) between chemical and enzymological data was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Pearson's correlation coefficient (r)

steps.description: The significance of the correlation is expressed by P value that is below 0.05 in analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate P value for correlation significance

steps.description: The significance of the correlation is expressed by P value that is below 0.05 in analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify correlations with a P value below 0.05 as statistically significant

steps.description: A sign preceding the coefficient of correlation (+ or −) indicates the direction of correlation.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret the direction of the correlation based on the sign of the Pearson's coefficient
